title: 3D split-KV dispatch for M>1 spec-verify
hardware: local AWS A10G (single in-container GPU) — EXPLORATORY, NOT official a10g-small
workload: 128 public bench prompts x 512 output tokens, seed 1, no precache (eval_prompts_sharegpt absent locally -> ungates without warming; identical for both arms)

mechanism_confirmed.guard: 1 < max_seqlen_q <= 64 (so prefill/PPL path, max_seqlen_q>64, never redirects)

tps_128prompt.metric: steady_gen_tps_mean = unweighted mean of vLLM's per-interval 'Avg generation throughput' readings (serve_profile.parse_spec_log); this is the meter PR #22/#39 used and the one comparable to the official 424.5 baseline
tps_128prompt.note: local relative gain robust to no-precache; absolute depressed vs official 424.5 (no precache locally)

wall_clock_cross_check.metric: token-weighted aggregate = num_completion_tokens / served duration_s (decode_*.summary.json); includes prefill+ramp+drain, robust at any batch size
wall_clock_cross_check.interpretation: patched serve finished the IDENTICAL 65,536-token workload in 144.3s vs 167.5s (+16.1%). Wall-clock CORROBORATES the steady-meter +10.86% and brackets it from above, so the headline is conservative.

tps_16prompt_pilot.note: 16-prompt pilot's steady_gen_tps_mean was INVERTED (baseline 325.85, patched 221.25, -32%) — an unweighted-per-interval artifact: at 16 prompts the patched arm finishes in ~3-4 log intervals so the low-throughput batch-DRAIN interval dominates the unweighted mean. The SAME pilot's wall-clock (baseline 23.380s, patched 19.022s) shows patched +22.9% FASTER, agreeing with the 128-prompt result. The pilot corroborates, it does not contradict; steady_gen_tps_mean is simply unreliable at tiny batch.

ppl_128prompt.verdict: UNAFFECTED (prefill chunks >64 tok never redirect; well under cap)

greedy.validity_anchor: M=1 AR (spec-off) reference for the submitted checkpoint (program.md gate)
greedy.interpretation_equidistant: patch is greedy-EQUIVALENT to baseline against the validity anchor (10 ~= 11); patch is INERT at M=1 so the reference itself is unchanged with/without the patch
greedy.determinism_clincher: baseline run1 vs run2 = 32/32 IDENTICAL -> stack is deterministic run-to-run; therefore patched-vs-baseline divergence is a DETERMINISTIC 3D-reduction-order shift (6.1e-5 op-level cascading over 512 int4 tokens), NOT FP noise
greedy.known_context: local M=1-AR gate is OVER-CONSERVATIVE: PR #32 found this same (leaderboard-VALID) submission DIVERGENT 27/32 against its own M1-AR ref; vLLM 0.22.x spec decode is greedy-DIVERGENT vs M1-AR at every precision (PR #5/#19/#24, vLLM #27433) yet the spec-decode frontier is leaderboard-valid. The official 'served gate' tolerates this.
greedy.refines_pr39_claim: PR #39 said 'split-KV is bit-identical attention, zero greedy risk'. Empirically the 3D path is within bf16 tolerance (6.1e-5) but NOT bit-identical, and DOES shift the served stream vs the 2D baseline (109/128). But it is greedy-EQUIVALENT to baseline against the validity anchor, so 'no added gate risk' holds in the validity-relevant sense.

projection_to_official.derivation: local +10.86% (steady meter) solves to ~50% saving of the 19.6% attention slice = PR #39 'conservative 2x' scenario
projection_to_official.caveat: projection only; local absolute steady-meter 428 < 440 gate due to no-precache depression (local wall-clock 454 does cross 440, see decision.tps_gate_metric_ambiguity)

decision.pr_gate: open HF approval issue IFF local TPS > 440 AND greedy gate passes
decision.tps_gate_metric_ambiguity: steady_gen_tps_mean = 428 < 440 (the meter comparable to the 424.5 baseline) BUT token-weighted wall-clock = 454 > 440. Both are no-precache-depressed local numbers (~9% below the official a10g-small), so neither cleanly satisfies a 440 gate that was framed against the OFFICIAL 424.5 baseline. The robust quantity is the RELATIVE +10.86%/+16.1%.
decision.reason: Both AND-conditions are at best unmet/ambiguous: (1) the greedy gate is clearly NOT 16/16 (10/128 vs M1-AR, a KNOWN over-conservative gate that fails the baseline too); (2) the TPS gate is metric-ambiguous (steady 428<440, wall-clock 454>440) and confounded by no-precache. Per my hard rule, escalating to an HF job is the advisor's call and requires human approval; I do NOT unilaterally open the approval issue on an ambiguous gate. Reporting the full picture (relative +10.86%/+16.1% -> ~471-493 projected official, greedy equivalent-to-baseline, PPL unaffected) and deferring the launch decision.
